reference.title: User Interface Reference
reference.shortdesc: Portfolio Manager uses a two-panel layout with a week navigator on the left and a six-tab notebook on the right. This topic describes each panel and tab.

section.title: Main Window Layout

image.alt: Annotated screenshot of the Portfolio Manager main window showing the left panel week navigator and right panel tab notebook
Main window: left panel shows the week navigator list; right panel shows six tabs: Dashboard, Sessions, Projects, Milestones, Weekly Review, and Settings.

section.p: The minimum window size is 1024 × 768 pixels. The window is resizable; both panels expand proportionally.

section.title: Left Panel — Week Navigator
section.p: The left panel displays a scrollable list of week keys in YYYY.W format. The list shows the 12 preceding weeks, the current week (highlighted), and the 4 following weeks. Clicking a week navigates the Sessions and Weekly Review tabs to that week.

section.title: Dashboard Tab
section.p: Displays a portfolio health snapshot for the selected week:

strow.stentry: The week key and date range (for example, "Week 2026.15 — Apr 13–19").
One row per active project. Columns: Name, Status, Planned sessions, Done sessions, Remaining sessions, Score.
Aggregate session counts and the portfolio score with a color-coded status badge.
Total planned and done session hours, and a list of upcoming milestones for the week.

section.title: Sessions Tab
section.p: Lists all sessions for the selected week and provides tools to create, edit, and update them:

strow.stentry: Text entry plus Previous and Next buttons to navigate weeks.
Columns: Date, Project, Milestone, Duration, Status, Session (label). Color-coded by status.
Opens the Session dialog to create a session.
Opens the Session dialog for the selected session.
Advances the selected session directly to Done status.
Removes the selected session after confirmation.
Shows planned hours, done hours, and remaining hours against the weekly budget.
Dropdown to show Active, Backlog, Archive, or All projects.
Columns: Name, Status, Priority, Started, Milestones (count), Sessions (count).
Opens the Project dialog to create a project.
Opens the Project dialog for the selected project, including the plan document editor.
Moves the selected project to Archive status after confirmation.
Permanently removes the project and all its sessions and milestones. Requires confirmation.
Selects the project whose milestones are displayed.
Columns: Description, Status, Target Date, Notes. Sorted by sort_order.
Opens the Milestone dialog for the selected milestone.
Removes the selected milestone after confirmation.
Left panel listing all saved reviews by week key. Click any entry to view that review.
Text entry and navigation buttons for the review week.
Loads or creates the review for the selected week.
Eight text fields for reflection (what moved, what stalled, signals, decision for next week) and planning (primary focus, project to deprioritize, risk to watch, first session target).
Logging verbosity. Options: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.
Pre-fills the duration field when creating a session. Range: 15–480.
Hours available per week for personal projects. Range: 1–100.
Read-only display of the current database file location. A restart is required after changing this path.
